name: Curcumin
description: Natural yellow from turmeric. Completely safe.

name: Riboflavin
description: Vitamin B2, yellow pigment. Essential nutrient.

name: Tartrazine
description: Synthetic yellow. Linked to hyperactivity in children.

name: Quinoline Yellow
description: Synthetic yellow. EU requires 'may affect activity in children'.

name: Sunset Yellow FCF
description: Synthetic orange-yellow. Associated with hyperactivity, allergies.

name: Cochineal
description: Red from cochineal insects. NOT vegetarian.

name: Erythrosine
description: Cherry red. Thyroid effects at high doses.

name: Allura Red AC
description: Most widely used synthetic color. Hyperactivity link.

name: Patent Blue V
description: Blue dye. Severe allergic reactions possible.

name: Brilliant Blue FCF
description: One of most studied food colors. Safe.

name: Ammonia Caramel
description: Linked to hyperactivity in children. EU restricted.

name: Annatto
description: Natural orange-yellow from achiote seeds. Safe.

name: Paprika Extract
description: Natural red-orange from peppers. Safe, contains vitamin A.

name: Lycopene
description: Natural red from tomatoes. Antioxidant. Safe.

name: Lutein
description: Yellow from marigold. Good for eye health.

name: Anthocyanins
description: Natural pigments in fruits. Antioxidants. Safe.

name: Titanium Dioxide
description: White pigment. Nano-particle concerns. EFSA says safe.

name: Iron Oxides
description: Iron-based pigments. Safe in food amounts.

name: Aluminium
description: Metallic powder on decorations. Neurological concerns.

name: Silver
description: Metallic silver on cake decorations. Safe.

name: Gold
description: Metallic gold decorations. Inert, not absorbed.

name: Potassium Sorbate
description: Widely used. Safe in food amounts.

name: Sodium Benzoate
description: Common benzoate. Can form benzene with vitamin C + heat.

name: Ethylparaben
description: Paraben. Endocrine concerns at high doses.

name: Nisin
description: Natural bacteriocin from milk. Completely safe.

name: Formaldehyde
description: Known carcinogen. Banned in most countries.

name: Sodium Nitrite
description: Preservative for meats. Converts to nitrosamines when heated.

name: Sodium Nitrate
description: Converts to nitrite. Restricted for vegetables.

name: Propionic Acid
description: Natural in breast milk. Used in bread.

name: TBHQ
description: Synthetic antioxidant. Liver concerns at high doses.

name: BHA
description: Antioxidant. High doses linked to cancer in animals.

name: BHT
description: Similar to BHA. Organ toxicity at very high doses.

name: Citric Acid
description: Natural in citrus fruits. Completely safe.

name: Phosphoric Acid
description: Soft drink acid. Bone density concerns.

name: Acesulfame Potassium
description: Zero-calorie sweetener 200x sweeter than sugar. Safe.

name: Isomalt
description: Sugar alcohol. Half calories of sugar.

name: Sucralose
description: 600x sweeter. Most studied sweetener. FDA approved 1999.

name: Steviol Glycosides
description: Natural from Stevia plant. 200-400x sweeter. Very safe.

name: Erythritol
description: Sugar alcohol 70% as sweet as sugar. Nearly zero cal.
sideEffects: digestive upset at very high doses

name: Maltitol
description: Sugar alcohol. Half sweetness, half calories.

name: Xylitol
description: 1:1 sweetness with sugar. Safe for teeth. Toxic to dogs.

name: Monosodium Glutamate
description: MSG. Safe for most. Chinese Restaurant Syndrome possible.
sideEffects: neurological effects at very high doses

name: Disodium Guanylate
description: Often paired with MSG. Gout flare-ups.

name: Disodium Inosinate
description: Often from animal sources. Check if vegetarian.

name: Carrageenan
description: From red seaweed. Controversial — gut inflammation concerns.

name: Locust Bean Gum
description: From carob seeds. Ice cream, sauces. Safe.

name: Guar Gum
description: From guar beans. May cause bloating at high doses.

name: Gum Arabic
description: From acacia trees. Soft drinks, confectionery. Safe.

name: Sorbitol
description: Sugar alcohol. Safe for diabetics. Digestive upset at high doses.

name: Pectins
description: Natural fiber from fruits. Thickener, gelling.

name: Gelatin
description: Animal protein from collagen. NOT vegetarian.

name: Sodium Pyrophosphates
description: Water retention in meats. Raises phosphorus intake.

name: Methyl Cellulose
sideEffects: digestive blockage risk if not taken with water

name: Hydroxypropyl Methyl Cellulose
description: Plant fiber derivative. Vegan gelatin substitute.

name: Acetic Acid Esters of Mono- and Diglycerides
Hydrochloric Acid
description: Cheese, gelatin. Safe in food amounts.

name: Potassium Chloride
sideEffects: blood pressure effects at high doses

name: Talc
description: Anti-caking. Respiratory concerns. Banned in some countries.

name: Shellac
description: Lac bug secretions coating. NOT vegetarian.

name: Potassium Bromate
description: Flour treatment. Banned in EU, India. Cancer risk.

name: Azodicarbonamide
description: Flour treatment. Banned in EU, Australia.

name: Lysozyme
description: Enzyme from egg whites. NOT vegetarian.

name: Polydextrose
sideEffects: digestive upset at very high doses

name: Propylene Glycol
description: Anti-caking in seasonings. CNS depression at high doses.

name: Asafoetida
description: Hing. Natural gum resin. Used in Indian cooking. Safe.

name: Mango Powder
description: Amchur. Dried green mango. Natural souring.

name: Kala Namak
description: Black salt with sulfur. Chaat, Indian cooking.

name: Nutmeg Extract
description: Hallucinogenic at high doses. Use sparingly.
